     9→
    10→Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
    11→distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    12→WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    13→See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14→limitations under the License.
    15→-/
    16→
    17→import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18→
    19→/-!
    20→# Union-closed sets conjecture
    21→
    22→*Reference:* [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Union-closed_sets_conjecture)
    23→
    24→In this file, we:
    25→* state the conjecture
    26→* state three solved variants of the conjecture, without proof
    27→* prove two solved variants of the conjecture
    28→* prove the conjecture is sharp
    29→-/
    30→
    31→open Finset
    32→
    33→variable {n : Type*} [DecidableEq n] {A : Finset (Finset n)}
    34→
    35→namespace UnionClosed
    36→
    37→abbrev IsUnionClosed (A : Finset (Finset n)) : Prop :=
    38→  ∀ᵉ (X ∈ A) (Y ∈ A), X ∪ Y ∈ A
    39→
    40→@[category API, AMS 5]
    41→lemma isUnionClosed_univ (n : Type*) [DecidableEq n] [Fintype n] :
    42→    IsUnionClosed (univ (α := Finset n)) := by
    43→  simp [IsUnionClosed]
    44→
    45→@[category API, AMS 5]
    46→lemma isUnionClosed_powerset (S : Finset n) : IsUnionClosed S.powerset := by
    47→  intro X hX Y hY
    48→  rw [Finset.mem_powerset] at hX hY ⊢
    49→  exact union_subset hX hY
    50→
    51→/--
    52→For every finite union-closed family of sets, other than the family containing only the empty set,
    53→there exists an element that belongs to at least half of the sets in the family.
    54→-/
    55→@[category research open, AMS 5]
    56→theorem union_closed
    57→    [Nonempty n]
    58→    (h_ne_singleton_empty : A ≠ {∅})
    59→    (h_union_closed : IsUnionClosed A) :
    60→    ∃ i : n, (1 / 2 : ℚ) * #A ≤ #{x ∈ A | i ∈ x} := by
    61→  sorry
    62→
    63→/--
    64→Yu [Yu23] showed that the union-closed sets conjecture holds with a constant of approximately
    65→0.38234 instead of 1/2.
    66→[Yu23] Yu, Lei (2023). "Dimension-free bounds for the union-closed sets conjecture". Entropy. 25 (5): 767.
    67→-/
    68→@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    69→theorem union_closed.variants.yu
    70→    [Nonempty n]
    71→    (h_ne_singleton_empty : A ≠ {∅})
    72→    (h_union_closed : IsUnionClosed A) :
    73→    ∃ i : n, (0.38234 : ℚ) * #A ≤ #{x ∈ A | i ∈ x} := by
    74→  sorry
    75→
    76→/--
    77→Vuckovic and Zivkovic [Vu17] showed that the union-closed sets conjecture holds for set families
    78→whose universal set has cardinality at most 12.
    79→[Vu17] Vuckovic, Bojan; Zivkovic, Miodrag (2017). "The 12-Element Case of Frankl's Conjecture" (PDF). IPSI BGD Transactions on Internet Research. 13 (1): 65.
    80→-/
    81→@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    82→theorem union_closed.variants.univ_card
    83→    [Fintype n] [Nonempty n]
    84→    (h_ne_singleton_empty : A ≠ {∅})
    85→    (h_union_closed : IsUnionClosed A)
    86→    (h_card : Fintype.card n ≤ 12) :
    87→    ∃ i : n, (1 / 2 : ℚ) * #A ≤ #{x ∈ A | i ∈ x} := by
    88→  sorry
    89→
    90→/--
    91→Roberts and Simpson [Ro10] showed that the union-closed sets conjecture holds for set families of
    92→size at most 46.
    93→Their method, however, combined with the result of [Vu17], further shows that it holds for `#A ≤ 50`
    94→as well.
    95→[Ro10] Roberts, Ian; Simpson, Jamie (2010). "A note on the union-closed sets conjecture" (PDF). Australas. J. Combin. 47: 265–267.
    96→[Vu17] Vuckovic, Bojan; Zivkovic, Miodrag (2017). "The 12-Element Case of Frankl's Conjecture" (PDF). IPSI BGD Transactions on Internet Research. 13 (1): 65.
    97→-/
    98→@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    99→theorem union_closed.variants.family_card
   100→    [Nonempty n]
   101→    (h_ne_singleton_empty : A ≠ {∅})
   102→    (h_union_closed : IsUnionClosed A)
   103→    (hA : #A ≤ 50) :
   104→    ∃ i : n, (1 / 2 : ℚ) * #A ≤ #{x ∈ A | i ∈ x} := by
   105→  sorry
   106→
   107→/--
   108→We can show the union-closed sets conjecture is true for the case where the universal set has
   109→cardinality 2, by brute force.
   110→-/
   111→@[category research solved, AMS 5]
   112→theorem union_closed.variants.univ_card_two (A : Finset (Finset (Fin 2)))
   113→    (h_ne_singleton_empty : A ≠ {∅})
   114→    (h_union_closed : IsUnionClosed A) :
   115→    ∃ i, (1 / 2 : ℚ) * #A ≤ #{x ∈ A | i ∈ x} := by
   116→  decide +revert +kernel
   117→
   118→/--
   119→We can show the union-closed sets conjecture is true for the case where the set family contains
   120→some singleton.
   121→-/
   122→@[category research solved, AMS 5]
   123→theorem union_closed.variants.singleton_mem
   124→    (h_union_closed : IsUnionClosed A)
   125→    (i : n) (hi : {i} ∈ A) :
   126→    ∃ i, (1 / 2 : ℚ) * #A ≤ #{x ∈ A | i ∈ x} := by
   127→  use i
   128→  set B : Finset (Finset n) := {x ∈ A | i ∉ x}
   129→  set C : Finset (Finset n) := {x ∈ A | i ∈ x}
   130→  have h₁ : (B : Set <| Finset n).InjOn (insert i) := by
   131→    simp only [Set.InjOn, coe_filter, Set.mem_setOf_eq, and_imp, B]
   132→    rintro x - hx y - hy hxy
   133→    have := congr(($hxy).erase i)
   134→    rwa [erase_insert hx, erase_insert hy] at this
   135→  have h₂ : (B : Set <| Finset n).MapsTo (insert i) C := by
   136→    simp only [Set.MapsTo, coe_filter, Set.mem_setOf_eq, mem_insert, true_or, and_true,
   137→      and_imp, B, C]
   138→    intro x hx hix
   139→    rw [Finset.insert_eq]
   140→    exact h_union_closed _ hi _ hx
   141→  have h₃ : #B ≤ #C := Finset.card_le_card_of_injOn _ h₂ h₁
   142→  have h₄ : #C + #B = #A := by rw [card_filter_add_card_filter_not]
   143→  have : #A ≤ 2 * #C := by omega
   144→  cancel_denoms
   145→  norm_cast
   146→
   147→/--
   148→The union-closed sets conjecture is sharp in the sense that if we replace the constant `1/2` with
   149→any larger constant, then the conjecture fails.
   150→-/
   151→@[category research solved, AMS 5]
   152→theorem union_closed.variants.sharpness [Fintype n] (c : ℝ) (hc : 1 / 2 < c) :
   153→    ¬ (∀ A : Finset (Finset n), A ≠ {∅} → IsUnionClosed A →
   154→        ∃ i : n, c * #A ≤ #{x ∈ A | i ∈ x}) := by
   155→  intro h
   156→  -- We can safely assume `n` is nonempty.
   157→  obtain hn | hn := isEmpty_or_nonempty n
   158→  · specialize h ∅
   159→    simp only [ne_eq, card_empty, CharP.cast_eq_zero, mul_zero, filter_empty, le_refl,
   160→      IsEmpty.exists_iff, imp_false, not_forall, notMem_empty, exists_const, Decidable.not_not] at h
   161→    have : ∅ ∈ (∅ : Finset (Finset n)) := by simp [h]
   162→    simp at this
   163→  -- Use A as the set of all subsets of `n`, which is not singleton empty and is union-closed.
   164→  let A : Finset (Finset n) := univ
   165→  have h_ne_singleton_empty : A ≠ {∅} := by
   166→    have : #A = 2 ^ Fintype.card n := by simp [A]
   167→    have : 1 < #A := by simp [this]
   168→    intro h
   169→    simp [h] at this
   170→  obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := h univ h_ne_singleton_empty (isUnionClosed_univ n)
   171→  have hn : 1 ≤ Fintype.card n := by
   172→    rw [Nat.add_one_le_iff]
   173→    exact Fintype.card_pos
   174→  -- Now the number of sets containing `i` is `2 ^ (Fintype.card n - 1)`
   175→  have : #{x : Finset n | i ∈ x} = 2 ^ (Fintype.card n - 1) := by
   176→    have : ({x : Finset n | i ∈ x} : Finset _) = (univ.erase i).powerset.image (insert i) := by
   177→      ext x
   178→      simp only [mem_filter, mem_univ, true_and, mem_image, mem_powerset, subset_erase, subset_univ]
   179→      constructor
   180→      · intro h
   181→        use x.erase i
   182→        simp [h]
   183→      · rintro ⟨_, _, rfl⟩
   184→        simp
   185→    rw [this, card_image_of_injOn, card_powerset]
   186→    · simp
   187→    intro a ha b hb h
   188→    simp only [coe_powerset, coe_erase, coe_univ, Set.mem_preimage, Set.mem_powerset_iff,
   189→      Set.subset_diff, Set.subset_univ, Set.disjoint_singleton_right, mem_coe, true_and] at ha hb
   190→    have := congr(($h).erase i)
   191→    rwa [erase_insert ha, erase_insert hb] at this
   192→  simp only [card_univ, Fintype.card_finset, Nat.cast_pow, Nat.cast_ofNat, this] at hi
   193→  rw [pow_sub₀ _ (by simp) hn] at hi
   194→  -- which is a contradiction.
   195→  have : (1 / 2 : ℚ) * 2 ^ (Fintype.card n) < c * 2 ^ (Fintype.card n) := by
   196→    gcongr
   197→    simpa using hc
   198→  have : (0 : ℝ) < 0 := by linear_combination this + hi
   199→  simp at this
   200→
   201→/--
   202→If the UC conjecture is tight for some family `A` then $|A| = 2^k$ for some $k$.
   203→
   204→Reference: Conjecture 3 in https://www.nieuwarchief.nl/serie5/pdf/naw5-2023-24-4-225.pdf.
   205→-/
   206→@[category research open, AMS 5]
   207→theorem union_closed.variants.cardinality_even_of_union_closed_tight
   208→    [Nonempty n] (hA : A ≠ {∅} ∧ A ≠ ∅) (hA : IsUnionClosed A)
   209→    (UCC_tight : ∀ i, #{x ∈ A | i ∈ x} = (1 / 2 : ℝ) * #A) :
   210→    ∃ k, #A = 2 ^ k := by
   211→  sorry
   212→
   213→end UnionClosed

    16→import Mathlib.Data.Set.Card
    17→
    18→/-!
    19→# VC dimension of a set family
    20→
    21→This file defines the VC dimension of a set family as the maximum size of a set it shatters.
    22→-/
    23→
    24→variable {α : Type*} {𝒜 ℬ : Set (Set α)} {A B : Set α} {a : α} {d d' : ℕ}
    25→
    26→variable (𝒜 A) in
    27→/-- A set family `𝒜` shatters a set `A` if restricting `𝒜` to `A` gives rise to all subsets of `A`.
    28→-/
    29→def Shatters : Prop := ∀ ⦃B⦄, B ⊆ A → ∃ C ∈ 𝒜, A ∩ C = B
    30→
    31→lemma Shatters.exists_inter_eq_singleton (hs : Shatters 𝒜 A) (ha : a ∈ A) : ∃ B ∈ 𝒜, A ∩ B = {a} :=
    32→  hs <| Set.singleton_subset_iff.2 ha
    33→
    34→lemma Shatters.mono_left (h : 𝒜 ⊆ ℬ) (h𝒜 : Shatters 𝒜 A) : Shatters ℬ A :=
    35→  fun _B hB ↦ let ⟨u, hu, hut⟩ := h𝒜 hB; ⟨u, h hu, hut⟩
    36→
    37→lemma Shatters.mono_right (h : B ⊆ A) (hs : Shatters 𝒜 A) : Shatters 𝒜 B := fun u hu ↦ by
    38→  obtain ⟨v, hv, rfl⟩ := hs (hu.trans h); exact ⟨v, hv, inf_congr_right hu <| inf_le_of_left_le h⟩
    39→
    40→lemma Shatters.exists_superset (h : Shatters 𝒜 A) : ∃ B ∈ 𝒜, A ⊆ B :=
    41→  let ⟨B, hB, hst⟩ := h .rfl; ⟨B, hB, Set.inter_eq_left.1 hst⟩
    42→
    43→lemma Shatters.of_forall_subset (h : ∀ B, B ⊆ A → B ∈ 𝒜) : Shatters 𝒜 A :=
    44→  fun B hB ↦ ⟨B, h _ hB, Set.inter_eq_right.2 hB⟩
    45→
    46→protected lemma Shatters.nonempty (h : Shatters 𝒜 A) : 𝒜.Nonempty :=
    47→  let ⟨B, hB, _⟩ := h .rfl; ⟨B, hB⟩
    48→
    49→@[simp] lemma shatters_empty : Shatters 𝒜 ∅ ↔ 𝒜.Nonempty :=
    50→  ⟨Shatters.nonempty, fun ⟨A, hs⟩ B hB ↦ ⟨A, hs, by simpa [eq_comm] using hB⟩⟩
    51→
    52→protected lemma Shatters.subset_iff (h : Shatters 𝒜 A) : B ⊆ A ↔ ∃ u ∈ 𝒜, A ∩ u = B :=
    53→  ⟨fun hB ↦ h hB, by rintro ⟨u, _, rfl⟩; exact Set.inter_subset_left⟩
    54→
    55→lemma shatters_iff : Shatters 𝒜 A ↔ (A ∩ ·) '' 𝒜 = A.powerset :=
    56→  ⟨fun h ↦ by ext B; simp [h.subset_iff], fun h B hB ↦ h.superset hB⟩
    57→
    58→lemma univ_shatters : Shatters .univ A := .of_forall_subset <| by simp
    59→
    60→@[simp] lemma shatters_univ : Shatters 𝒜 .univ ↔ 𝒜 = .univ := by
    61→  simp [Shatters, Set.eq_univ_iff_forall]
    62→
    63→variable (𝒜 d) in
    64→/-- A set family `𝒜` has VC dimension at most `d` if there are no families `x` of elements indexed
    65→by `[d + 1]` and `A` of sets of `𝒜` indexed by `2^[d + 1]` such that `x i ∈ A s ↔ i ∈ s` for all
    66→`i ∈ [d + 1], s ⊆ [d + 1]`. -/
    67→def HasVCDimAtMost : Prop :=
    68→  ∀ (x : Fin (d + 1) → α) (A : Set (Fin (d + 1)) → Set α),
    69→    (∀ s, A s ∈ 𝒜) → ¬ ∀ i s, x i ∈ A s ↔ i ∈ s
    70→
    71→lemma HasVCDimAtMost.anti (h𝒜ℬ : 𝒜 ≤ ℬ) (hℬ : HasVCDimAtMost ℬ d) : HasVCDimAtMost 𝒜 d :=
    72→  fun _x _A hA ↦ hℬ _ _ fun _s ↦ h𝒜ℬ <| hA _
    73→
    74→lemma HasVCDimAtMost.mono (h : d ≤ d') (hd : HasVCDimAtMost 𝒜 d) : HasVCDimAtMost 𝒜 d' := by
    75→  rintro x A hA hxA
    76→  replace h : d + 1 ≤ d' + 1 := by omega
    77→  exact hd (x ∘ Fin.castLE h) (A ∘ Set.image (Fin.castLE h)) (by simp [hA]) fun i s ↦
    78→    (hxA ..).trans <| by simp
    79→
    80→@[simp] lemma HasVCDimAtMost.empty : HasVCDimAtMost (∅ : Set (Set α)) d := by simp [HasVCDimAtMost]
    81→
    82→proof_wanted hasVCDimAtMost_iff_shatters : HasVCDimAtMost 𝒜 d ↔ ∀ A, Shatters 𝒜 A → A.encard ≤ d
    83→

    16→import FormalConjecturesForMathlib.Combinatorics.SetFamily.VCDim
    17→import Mathlib.Algebra.Group.Action.Pointwise.Set.Basic
    18→
    19→/-!
    20→# VC dimension in a group
    21→
    22→This file defines the Vapnik–Chervonenkis (aka VC) dimension of a set in a group, defined as the
    23→VC dimension of its family of translates.
    24→
    25→It also defines the VCₙ dimension, which has no set family analogue.
    26→-/
    27→
    28→open scoped Pointwise
    29→
    30→variable {G : Type*} [CommGroup G] {A B : Set G} {n d d' : ℕ}
    31→
    32→variable (A d) in
    33→/-- A set `A` in an abelian group has VC dimension at most `d` iff one cannot find two sequences
    34→`x` and `y` of elements indexed by `[d + 1]` and `2 ^ [d + 1]` respectively such that
    35→`y s * x i ∈ A ↔ i ∈ s` for all `i ∈ [d + 1]`, `s ⊆ [d + 1]`. -/
    36→@[to_additive
    37→/-- A set `A` in an abelian group has VC dimension at most `d` iff one cannot find two sequences
    38→`x` and `y` of elements indexed by `[d + 1]` and `2 ^ [d + 1]` respectively such that
    39→`y s + x i ∈ A ↔ i ∈ s` for all `i ∈ [d + 1]`, `s ⊆ [d + 1]`. -/]
    40→def HasMulVCDimAtMost : Prop :=
    41→  ∀ (x : Fin (d + 1) → G) (y : Set (Fin (d + 1)) → G), ¬ ∀ i s, y s * x i ∈ A ↔ i ∈ s
    42→
    43→@[to_additive]
    44→lemma HasMulVCDimAtMost.mono (h : d ≤ d') (hd : HasMulVCDimAtMost A d) :
    45→    HasMulVCDimAtMost A d' := by
    46→  rintro x y hxy
    47→  replace h : d + 1 ≤ d' + 1 := by omega
    48→  exact hd (x ∘ Fin.castLE h) (y ∘ Set.image (Fin.castLE h)) fun i s ↦ (hxy ..).trans <| by simp
    49→
    50→@[to_additive (attr := simp)]
    51→lemma hasMulVCDimAtMost_compl : HasMulVCDimAtMost Aᶜ d ↔ HasMulVCDimAtMost A d :=
    52→  forall_congr' fun x ↦ (compl_involutive.toPerm.arrowCongr <| .refl _).forall_congr fun y ↦
    53→    not_congr <| forall_congr' fun i ↦ compl_involutive.toPerm.forall_congr <| by simp; tauto
    54→
    55→@[to_additive]
    56→protected alias ⟨HasMulVCDimAtMost.of_compl, HasMulVCDimAtMost.compl⟩ := hasMulVCDimAtMost_compl
    57→
    58→@[to_additive (attr := simp)]
    59→protected lemma HasMulVCDimAtMost.empty : HasMulVCDimAtMost (∅ : Set G) d := by
    60→  simpa [HasMulVCDimAtMost] using ⟨default, .univ, by simp⟩
    61→
    62→@[to_additive (attr := simp)]
    63→protected lemma HasMulVCDimAtMost.univ : HasMulVCDimAtMost (.univ : Set G) d := by
    64→  simpa [HasMulVCDimAtMost] using ⟨default, ∅, by simp⟩
    65→
    66→@[to_additive (attr := simp)]
    67→lemma hasVCDimAtMost_smul : HasVCDimAtMost {t • A | t : G} d ↔ HasMulVCDimAtMost A d := by
    68→  simpa [HasVCDimAtMost, HasMulVCDimAtMost, Classical.skolem (b := fun _ ↦ G), ← funext_iff,
    69→    Set.mem_smul_set_iff_inv_smul_mem]
    70→    using forall_congr' fun x ↦ (Equiv.inv _).forall_congr <| by simp
    71→
    72→variable (A d n) in
    73→/-- A set `A` in an abelian group has VCₙ dimension at most `d` iff one cannot find two sequences
    74→`x` and `y` of elements indexed by `[n] × [d + 1]` and `2 ^ [d + 1]ⁿ` respectively such that
    75→`y s * ∏ k, x (k, i k) ∈ A ↔ i ∈ s` for all `i ∈ [d + 1]ⁿ`, `s ⊆ [d + 1]ⁿ`. -/
    76→@[to_additive
    77→/-- A set `A` in an abelian group has VCₙ dimension at most `d` iff one cannot find two sequences
    78→`x` and `y` of elements indexed by `[n] × [d + 1]` and `2 ^ [d + 1]ⁿ` respectively such that
    79→`y s + ∑ k, x (k, i k) ∈ A ↔ i ∈ s` for all `i ∈ [d + 1]ⁿ`, `s ⊆ [d + 1]ⁿ`. -/]
    80→def HasMulVCNDimAtMost : Prop :=
    81→  ∀ (x : Fin n → Fin (d + 1) → G) (y : Set (Fin n → Fin (d + 1)) → G),
    82→    ¬ ∀ i s, y s * ∏ k, x k (i k) ∈ A ↔ i ∈ s
    83→
    84→@[to_additive]
    85→lemma HasMulVCNDimAtMost.mono (h : d ≤ d') (hd : HasMulVCNDimAtMost A n d) :
    86→    HasMulVCNDimAtMost A n d' := by
    87→  rintro x y hxy
    88→  replace h : d + 1 ≤ d' + 1 := by omega
    89→  exact hd (x · ∘ Fin.castLE h) (y ∘ Set.image (Fin.castLE h ∘ ·)) fun i s ↦
    90→    (hxy ..).trans <| by simp [funext_iff]; simp [← funext_iff]
    91→
    92→@[to_additive (attr := simp)]
    93→lemma hasMulVCNDimAtMost_compl : HasMulVCNDimAtMost Aᶜ n d ↔ HasMulVCNDimAtMost A n d :=
    94→  forall_congr' fun x ↦ (compl_involutive.toPerm.arrowCongr <| .refl _).forall_congr fun y ↦
    95→    not_congr <| forall_congr' fun i ↦ compl_involutive.toPerm.forall_congr <| by simp; tauto
    96→
    97→@[to_additive]
    98→protected alias ⟨HasMulVCNDimAtMost.of_compl, HasMulVCNDimAtMost.compl⟩ := hasMulVCNDimAtMost_compl
    99→
   100→@[to_additive (attr := simp)]
   101→protected lemma HasMulVCNDimAtMost.empty : HasMulVCNDimAtMost (∅ : Set G) n d := by
   102→  simpa [HasMulVCNDimAtMost] using ⟨default, .univ, by simp⟩
   103→
   104→@[to_additive (attr := simp)]
   105→protected lemma HasMulVCNDimAtMost.univ : HasMulVCNDimAtMost (.univ : Set G) n d := by
   106→  simpa [HasMulVCNDimAtMost] using ⟨default, ∅, by simp⟩
   107→
   108→@[to_additive (attr := simp)]
   109→lemma hasMulVCNDimAtMost_one : HasMulVCNDimAtMost A 1 d ↔ HasMulVCDimAtMost A d := by
   110→  symm
   111→  refine (Equiv.funUnique ..).symm.forall_congr fun x ↦
   112→    ((Equiv.Set.congr <| Equiv.funUnique ..).arrowCongr <| .refl _).symm.forall_congr fun y ↦
   113→    not_congr <| (Equiv.funUnique ..).symm.forall_congr fun i ↦
   114→    (Equiv.Set.congr <| Equiv.funUnique ..).symm.forall_congr fun s ↦ ?_
   115→  simp [Set.image_image, funext_iff]
   116→

    26→/--
    27→A set $A$ is said to be sum-free if the sumset $A + A$ is disjoint from $A$, i.e.
    28→if the equation $a + b = c$ has no solution with $a, b, c \in A$.
    29→-/
    30→def IsSumFree (A : Set α) : Prop := Disjoint (A + A) A
    31→
    32→/-- A set $A$ of natural numbers is said to have bounded gaps if there exists an integer $p$ such
    33→that $A ∩ [n, n + 1, ..., n + p]$ is nonempty for all $n$. -/
    34→def IsSyndetic (A : Set ℕ) : Prop := ∃ p, ∀ n, (A ∩ .Icc n (n + p)).Nonempty
    35→
    36→/-- A Sidon set is a set, such that such that all pairwise sums of elements are distinct apart from
    37→coincidences forced by the commutativity of addition. -/
    38→def IsSidon (A : Set α) : Prop := ∀ᵉ (i₁ ∈ A) (j₁ ∈ A) (i₂ ∈ A) (j₂ ∈ A),
    39→  i₁ + i₂ = j₁ + j₂ → (i₁ = j₁ ∧ i₂ = j₂) ∨ (i₁ = j₂ ∧ i₂ = j₁)
    40→

    43→lemma IsSidon.avoids_isAPOfLength_three {A : Set ℕ} (hA : IsSidon A)
    44→    {Y : Set ℕ} (hY : Y.IsAPOfLength 3) :
    45→    (A ∩ Y).ncard ≤ 2 := by
    46→  simp [IsAPOfLength, IsAPOfLengthWith] at hY
    47→  obtain ⟨hc, ⟨a, d, hY⟩⟩ := hY
    48→  have hY_card : Y.ncard = 3 := by simp [ncard, hc]
    49→  by_contra! h

    51→    have hY_fin : Finite Y := finite_of_ncard_ne_zero (by linarith)
    52→    rw [Set.eq_of_subset_of_ncard_le (Set.inter_subset_right) (by linarith)]
    53→  have ha : a ∈ A := mem_of_mem_inter_left <| hss (hY ▸ ⟨0, by norm_num, by simp⟩)
    54→  have ha₁ : a + d ∈ A := mem_of_mem_inter_left <| hss (hY ▸ ⟨1, by norm_num, by simp⟩)
    55→  have ha₂ : a + 2 • d ∈ A := mem_of_mem_inter_left <| hss (hY ▸ ⟨2, by norm_num, by simp⟩)
    56→  have := hA _ ha _ ha₁ _ ha₂ _ ha₁ (by simp; omega)
    57→  simp at this
    58→  simp [hY, this.1, setOf_and] at hY_card
    59→  linarith [ncard_singleton _ ▸ ncard_inter_le_ncard_right {a | ∃ x, x < 3} {a}]
    60→
    61→theorem IsSidon.subset {A B : Set α} (hB : IsSidon B) (hAB : A ⊆ B) : IsSidon A :=
    62→  fun _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ ↦ hB _ (hAB ‹_›) _ (hAB ‹_›) _ (hAB ‹_›) _ (hAB ‹_›) ‹_›
    63→
    64→theorem IsSidon.insert {A : Set α} {m : α} [IsRightCancelAdd α] [IsLeftCancelAdd α]
    65→    (hA : IsSidon A) :
    66→    IsSidon (A ∪ {m}) ↔ (m ∈ A ∨ ∀ᵉ (a ∈ A) (b ∈ A), m + m ≠ a + b ∧ ∀ c ∈ A, m + a ≠ b + c) := by
    67→  by_cases h_mem : m ∈ A
    68→  · exact ⟨fun _ ↦ .inl h_mem, fun _ ↦ by rwa [union_singleton, insert_eq_of_mem h_mem]⟩
    69→  refine ⟨fun h ↦ .inr fun a ha b hb ↦ ⟨fun hc ↦ ?_, fun c hc h_contr ↦ ?_⟩, fun hm ↦ ?_⟩
    70→  · exact h m (by simp) a (by simp [ha]) m (by simp) b (by simp [hb]) hc
    71→      |>.elim (fun _ ↦ by simp_all) (fun _ ↦ by simp_all)
    72→  · exact h m (by simp) b (by simp [hb]) a (by simp [ha]) c (by simp [hc]) h_contr
    73→      |>.elim (fun _ ↦ by simp_all) (fun _ ↦ by simp_all)
    74→  · intro i₁ hi₁
    75→    rcases hi₁ with (hi₁ | hi₁)
    76→    · intro j₁ hj₁
    77→      rcases hj₁ with (hj₁ | hj₁)
    78→      · intro i₂ hi₂
    79→        rcases hi₂ with (hi₂ | hi₂)
    80→        · intro j₂ hj₂
    81→          rcases hj₂ with (hj₂ | hj₂)
    82→          · exact fun h ↦ hA i₁ hi₁ j₁ hj₁ i₂ hi₂ j₂ hj₂ h
    83→          · simp_all
    84→            exact fun h ↦ by cases (hm j₁ hj₁ i₁ hi₁).2 i₂ hi₂ (add_comm j₁ m ▸ h.symm)
    85→        · simp_all
    86→          exact fun a ha h ↦ by cases (hm i₁ hi₁ j₁ hj₁).2 a ha (add_comm i₁ m ▸ h)
    87→      · simp_all
    88→        refine ⟨fun b hb h ↦ .inr <| by simp_all [add_comm], fun b hb ↦ ⟨fun h ↦ ?_, ?_⟩⟩
    89→        · cases (hm i₁ hi₁ b hb).1 h.symm
    90→        · exact fun c hc h ↦ by cases ((hm c hc i₁ hi₁).2 b hb) h.symm
    91→    · simp_all
    92→      exact fun _ _ _ _ _ ↦ by simp_all [add_comm]
    93→
    94→
    95→/-!
    96→Maximal Sidon sets in an interval.
    97→
    98→We follow the convention that `IsMaximalSidonSetIn A N` means `A ⊆ {1, …, N}` is Sidon and
    99→is inclusion-maximal among subsets of `Set.Icc 1 N` with the Sidon property.
   100→-/
   101→
   102→/-- `IsMaximalSidonSetIn A N` means `A ⊆ {1, …, N}` is Sidon and cannot be extended within
   103→`{1, …, N}` while remaining Sidon. -/
   104→def IsMaximalSidonSetIn (A : Set ℕ) (N : ℕ) : Prop :=
   105→  A ⊆ Set.Icc 1 N ∧ IsSidon A ∧
   106→    ∀ ⦃x : ℕ⦄, x ∈ Set.Icc 1 N → x ∉ A → ¬ IsSidon (A ∪ {x})
   107→
   108→namespace IsMaximalSidonSetIn
   109→
   110→/-- If `A` is a maximal Sidon set in `{1, …, N}`, then `A ⊆ {1, …, N}`. -/
   111→theorem subset {A : Set ℕ} {N : ℕ} (hA : IsMaximalSidonSetIn A N) :
   112→    A ⊆ Set.Icc 1 N := hA.1
   113→
   114→/-- If `A` is a maximal Sidon set in `{1, …, N}`, then `A` is Sidon. -/
   115→theorem isSidon {A : Set ℕ} {N : ℕ} (hA : IsMaximalSidonSetIn A N) : IsSidon A := hA.2.1
   116→
   117→/-- Maximality condition unpacked. -/
   118→theorem maximal {A : Set ℕ} {N : ℕ} (hA : IsMaximalSidonSetIn A N) {x : ℕ}
   119→    (hx : x ∈ Set.Icc 1 N) (hxA : x ∉ A) : ¬ IsSidon (A ∪ {x}) := hA.2.2 hx hxA
   120→
   121→end IsMaximalSidonSetIn
   122→
   123→end Set
   124→
   125→namespace Finset
   126→
   127→instance (A : Finset α) [DecidableEq α] : Decidable (IsSidon (A : Set α)) :=
   128→  decidable_of_iff (∀ᵉ (i₁ ∈ A) (j₁ ∈ A) (i₂ ∈ A) (j₂ ∈ A), _) <| by rfl
   129→
   130→/-- The maximum size of a Sidon set in the supplied `Finset`. -/
   131→def maxSidonSubsetCard (A : Finset α) [DecidableEq α] : ℕ :=
   132→  (A.powerset.filter fun B : Finset α ↦ IsSidon (B : Set α)).sup Finset.card
   133→
   134→/-- If `A` is finite Sidon, then `A ∪ {s}` is also Sidon provided `s ≥ A.max + 1`. -/
   135→theorem IsSidon.insert_ge_max' {A : Finset ℕ} (h : A.Nonempty) (hA : IsSidon (A : Set ℕ)) {s : ℕ}
   136→    (hs : 2 * A.max' h + 1 ≤ s) :
   137→    IsSidon (A ∪ {s}) := by
   138→  have h₁ {a b c : ℕ} (ha : a ∈ A) (hb : b ∈ A) (hc : c ∈ A) :
   139→        a + b < 2 * A.max' h + 1 + c := by linarith [A.le_max' _ ha, A.le_max' _ hb]
   140→  have : s ∉ A := by
   141→    exact mt (A.le_max' _) <| not_le.2 <| Finset.max'_lt_iff _ ‹_› |>.2 fun a ha ↦ by
   142→      linarith [A.le_max' _ ha]
   143→  exact (IsSidon.insert hA).2 <| by simpa [this] using fun a ha b hb ↦
   144→    ⟨by linarith [A.le_max' _ ha, A.le_max' _ hb], fun c hc ↦ by linarith [h₁ hc hb ha]⟩
   145→
   146→theorem IsSidon.exists_insert {A : Finset ℕ} (h : A.Nonempty) (hA : IsSidon (A : Set ℕ)) :
   147→    ∃ m ∉ A, IsSidon (A ∪ {m}) := by
   148→  refine ⟨2 * A.max' h + 1, ?_, insert_ge_max' h hA le_rfl⟩
   149→  exact mt (A.le_max' _) <| not_le.2 <| Finset.max'_lt_iff _ ‹_› |>.2 fun a ha ↦ by
   150→    linarith [A.le_max' _ ha]
   151→
   152→theorem IsSidon.exists_insert_ge {A : Finset ℕ} (h : A.Nonempty) (hA : IsSidon (A : Set ℕ)) (s : ℕ) :
   153→    ∃ m ≥ s, m ∉ A ∧ IsSidon (A ∪ {m}) := by
   154→  refine ⟨if s ≥ 2 * A.max' h + 1 then s else 2 * A.max' h + 1, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
   155→  · split_ifs <;> linarith
   156→  · split_ifs <;>
   157→    exact mt (A.le_max' _) <| not_le.2 <| Finset.max'_lt_iff _ ‹_› |>.2 fun a ha ↦ by
   158→      linarith [A.le_max' _ ha]
   159→  · split_ifs with hs
   160→    · exact insert_ge_max' h hA hs
   161→    · exact insert_ge_max' h hA le_rfl
   162→
   163→end Finset
   164→

    19→import Mathlib.Order.CompletePartialOrder

    21→
    22→def Triplewise {α : Type*} (r : α → α → α → Prop) : Prop :=
    23→  ∀ ⦃i j k ⦄, i ≠ j → j ≠ k → i ≠ k → r i j k
    24→
    25→namespace Set
    26→
    27→variable {α : Type*} {r : α → α → α → Prop} {s t : Set α} {x y z : α}
    28→
    29→/--
    30→The ternary relation `r` holds triplewise on the set `s`
    31→if `r x y z` for all *distinct* `x y z ∈ s`.
    32→-/
    33→protected def Triplewise (s : Set α) (r : α → α → α → Prop) : Prop :=
    34→  ∀ ⦃x⦄, x ∈ s → ∀ ⦃y⦄, y ∈ s → ∀ ⦃z⦄, z ∈ s → x ≠ y → y ≠ z → x ≠ z → r x y z
    35→
    36→protected theorem Triplewise.eq (hs : s.Triplewise r)
    37→    (hx : x ∈ s) (hy : y ∈ s) (hz : z ∈ s) (h : ¬r x y z) :
    38→    x = y ∨ y = z ∨ x = z := by
    39→  have := (h <| hs hx hy hz · · ·)
    40→  tauto
    41→
    42→theorem Triplewise.mono (h : t ⊆ s) (hs : s.Triplewise r) :
    43→    t.Triplewise r :=
    44→  fun _ hx _ hy _ hz hxy hyz hxz ↦ hs (h hx) (h hy) (h hz) hxy hyz hxz
    45→
    46→@[simp]
    47→theorem Triplewise_univ_iff :
    48→    Set.univ.Triplewise r ↔ Triplewise r := by
    49→  simp [Set.Triplewise, Triplewise]
    50→
    51→theorem triplewise_of_encard_lt (r : α → α → α → Prop)
    52→    (h : s.encard < 3) : s.Triplewise r := by
    53→  unfold Set.Triplewise
    54→  contrapose! h
    55→  obtain ⟨x, hx, y, hy, z, hz, hxy, hyz, hxz, _⟩ := h
    56→  trans encard {x, y, z}
    57→  · simp [Set.encard_insert_of_notMem, *]
    58→    norm_num
    59→  · exact encard_le_encard_of_injOn (by simp [MapsTo, hx, hy, hz]) (injOn_id _)
    60→
    61→@[simp]
    62→theorem triplewise_empty (r : α → α → α → Prop) :
    63→    Set.Triplewise ∅ r :=
    64→  (notMem_empty · · |>.elim)
    65→
    66→@[simp]
    67→theorem triplewise_singleton (r : α → α → α → Prop) :
    68→    Set.Triplewise {x} r := by
    69→  apply triplewise_of_encard_lt
    70→  simp
    71→
    72→@[simp]
    73→theorem triplewise_pair
    74→    (r : α → α → α → Prop) : Set.Triplewise {x, y} r := by
    75→  apply triplewise_of_encard_lt
    76→  apply (encard_insert_le {y} x).trans_lt
    77→  simp
    78→  norm_num
    79→
    80→theorem triplewise_insert :
    81→    (insert x s).Triplewise r ↔ s.Triplewise r ∧
    82→    ∀ y ∈ s, ∀ z ∈ s, x ≠ y → x ≠ z → y ≠ z → r x y z ∧ r y x z ∧ r y z x := by
    83→  refine ⟨fun hxs ↦ ?_, fun ⟨hs, hxs⟩ ↦ ?_⟩
    84→  · refine ⟨hxs.mono (subset_insert x s), fun y hy z hz hxy hxz hyz ↦ ⟨?_, ?_, ?_⟩⟩
    85→    · exact hxs (mem_insert x s) (mem_insert_of_mem x hy) (mem_insert_of_mem x hz) hxy hyz hxz
    86→    · exact hxs (mem_insert_of_mem x hy) (mem_insert x s) (mem_insert_of_mem x hz) hxy.symm hxz hyz
    87→    · exact hxs (mem_insert_of_mem x hy) (mem_insert_of_mem x hz)
    88→        (mem_insert x s) hyz hxz.symm hxy.symm
    89→  · intro w hw y hy z hz hwy hyz hwz
    90→    rw [mem_insert_iff] at hw hy hz
    91→    obtain rfl | hw := hw
    92→    · refine (hxs y ?_ z ?_ hwy hwz hyz).1
    93→      · simpa [hwy.symm] using hy
    94→      · simpa [hwz.symm] using hz
    95→    obtain rfl | hy := hy
    96→    · exact (hxs w hw z (by simpa [hyz.symm] using hz) hwy.symm hyz hwz).2.1
    97→    obtain rfl | hz := hz
    98→    · exact (hxs w hw y hy hwz.symm hyz.symm hwy).2.2
    99→    exact hs hw hy hz hwy hyz hwz
   100→
   101→theorem triplewise_insert_of_not_mem (hx : x ∉ s) :
   102→    (insert x s).Triplewise r ↔ s.Triplewise r ∧
   103→    ∀ y ∈ s, ∀ z ∈ s, y ≠ z → r x y z ∧ r y x z ∧ r y z x := by
   104→  refine Set.triplewise_insert.trans <| and_congr_right' <| forall₄_congr ?_
   105→  intro y hy z hz
   106→  simp [(ne_of_mem_of_not_mem hy hx).symm, (ne_of_mem_of_not_mem hz hx).symm]
   107→
   108→protected theorem Triplewise.insert (hs : s.Triplewise r)
   109→    (h : ∀ y ∈ s, ∀ z ∈ s, x ≠ y → x ≠ z → y ≠ z → r x y z ∧ r y x z ∧ r y z x) :
   110→    (insert x s).Triplewise r :=
   111→  Set.triplewise_insert.mpr ⟨hs, h⟩
   112→
   113→protected theorem Triplewise.insert_of_not_mem
   114→    (hx : x ∉ s) (hs : s.Triplewise r)
   115→    (h : ∀ y ∈ s, ∀ z ∈ s, y ≠ z → r x y z ∧ r y x z ∧ r y z x) :
   116→    (insert x s).Triplewise r :=
   117→  (Set.triplewise_insert_of_not_mem hx).mpr ⟨hs, h⟩
   118→
   119→theorem triplewise_set_pred_iff {S : Set α} {P : Set α → Prop} :
   120→    S.Triplewise (P {·, ·, ·}) ↔ ∀ T ⊆ S, T.ncard = 3 → P T := by
   121→  constructor
   122→  · intro h T hT T_ncard
   123→    obtain ⟨x, y, z, hxy, hxz, hyz, rfl⟩ := ncard_eq_three.mp T_ncard
   124→    simp_rw [insert_subset_iff, singleton_subset_iff] at hT
   125→    exact h hT.1 hT.2.1 hT.2.2 hxy hyz hxz
   126→  · intro h x hx y hy z hz hxy hyz hxz
   127→    refine h _ ?_ (ncard_eq_three.mpr ⟨x, y, z, hxy, hxz, hyz, rfl⟩)
   128→    simp [insert_subset_iff, hx, hy, hz]
   129→
   130→end Set
   131→

    19→/-!
    20→# Erdős Problem 702
    21→
    22→*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/702](https://www.erdosproblems.com/702)
    23→
    24→If a family of k-element subsets of {1,...,n} has more than C(n-2,k-2) members (where k ≥ 4),
    25→then some two members of the family intersect in exactly one element.
    26→
    27→[Fr77] Frankl, P., *On families of finite sets no two of which intersect in a singleton*,
    28→Bull. Austral. Math. Soc. 17 (1977), 125–134.
    29→-/
    30→
    31→open Finset
    32→
    33→namespace Erdos702
    34→
    35→/--
    36→**Erdős–Sós Conjecture.** Let $k \geq 4$. If $\mathcal{F}$ is a family of $k$-element subsets of
    37→$\{1, \ldots, n\}$ with $|\mathcal{F}| > \binom{n-2}{k-2}$, then there exist distinct
    38→$A, B \in \mathcal{F}$ such that $|A \cap B| = 1$.
    39→
    40→Proved by Katona (unpublished) for $k = 4$, and by Frankl [Fr77] for all $k \geq 4$.
    41→-/
    42→@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    43→theorem erdos_702 (n k : ℕ) (hk : 4 ≤ k) (hkn : k ≤ n)
    44→    (F : Finset (Finset (Fin n)))
    45→    (hF_unif : ∀ A ∈ F, A.card = k)
    46→    (hF_large : F.card > Nat.choose (n - 2) (k - 2)) :
    47→    ∃ A ∈ F, ∃ B ∈ F, A ≠ B ∧ (A ∩ B).card = 1 := by
    48→  sorry
    49→
    50→end Erdos702
    51→

    16→
    17→import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18→
    19→/-!
    20→# Erdős Problem 703
    21→
    22→*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/703](https://www.erdosproblems.com/703)
    23→
    24→Let $T(n, r)$ be the maximum size of a family of subsets of $\{1, \ldots, n\}$ such that no two
    25→members intersect in exactly $r$ elements. The conjecture, proved by Frankl and Rödl, asserts that
    26→for every $\varepsilon > 0$ there exists $\delta > 0$ such that $T(n, r) < (2 - \delta)^n$ whenever
    27→$\varepsilon n < r < (1/2 - \varepsilon) n$.
    28→
    29→[FrRo87] Frankl, P. and Rödl, V., *Forbidden intersections*, Trans. Amer. Math. Soc. 300 (1987),
    30→259–286.
    31→-/
    32→
    33→open Finset
    34→
    35→namespace Erdos703
    36→
    37→/-- $T(n, r)$ is the maximum cardinality of a family $\mathcal{F}$ of subsets of
    38→$\{0, \ldots, n-1\}$ such that $|A \cap B| \neq r$ for all $A, B \in \mathcal{F}$. -/
    39→noncomputable def T (n r : ℕ) : ℕ :=
    40→  sSup {k : ℕ | ∃ F : Finset (Finset (Fin n)),
    41→    F.card = k ∧
    42→    ∀ A ∈ F, ∀ B ∈ F, (A ∩ B).card ≠ r}
    43→
    44→/--
    45→Erdős Problem 703 (Frankl–Rödl theorem):
    46→
    47→Let $r \geq 1$ and define $T(n,r)$ to be the maximum size of a family $\mathcal{F}$ of subsets
    48→of $\{1, \ldots, n\}$ such that $|A \cap B| \neq r$ for all $A, B \in \mathcal{F}$.
    49→
    50→For every $\varepsilon > 0$ there exists $\delta > 0$ such that for all $n$ and $r$ with
    51→$\varepsilon n < r < (1/2 - \varepsilon) n$, we have $T(n,r) < (2 - \delta)^n$.
    52→
    53→Proved by Frankl and Rödl [FrRo87].
    54→-/
    55→@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    56→theorem erdos_703 :
    57→    ∀ ε : ℝ, ε > 0 →
    58→    ∃ δ : ℝ, δ > 0 ∧
    59→      ∀ n : ℕ, ∀ r : ℕ,
    60→        ε * (n : ℝ) < (r : ℝ) →
    61→        (r : ℝ) < (1 / 2 - ε) * (n : ℝ) →
    62→        (T n r : ℝ) < (2 - δ) ^ n := by
    63→  sorry
    64→
    65→end Erdos703
    66→

    15→-/
    16→
    17→import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18→
    19→/-!
    20→# Erdős Problem 704
    21→
    22→*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/704](https://www.erdosproblems.com/704)
    23→
    24→Let $G_n$ be the unit distance graph in $\mathbb{R}^n$, with two vertices joined by an edge
    25→if and only if the Euclidean distance between them is $1$.
    26→
    27→Estimate the chromatic number $\chi(G_n)$. Does it grow exponentially in $n$? Does
    28→$\lim_{n \to \infty} \chi(G_n)^{1/n}$ exist?
    29→
    30→A generalisation of the Hadwiger–Nelson problem (which addresses $n = 2$).
    31→
    32→**Known results:**
    33→- Frankl–Wilson [FrWi81]: $\chi(G_n) \geq (1+o(1)) \cdot 1.2^n$
    34→- Larman–Rogers [LaRo72]: $\chi(G_n) \leq (3+o(1))^n$
    35→
    36→[FrWi81] Frankl, P. and Wilson, R. M., _Intersection theorems with geometric consequences_.
    37→Combinatorica 1 (1981), 357–368.
    38→
    39→[LaRo72] Larman, D. G. and Rogers, C. A., _The realization of distances within sets in
    40→Euclidean space_. Mathematika 19 (1972), 1–24.
    41→-/
    42→
    43→open Filter
    44→
    45→open scoped Topology
    46→
    47→namespace Erdos704
    48→
    49→/-- The unit distance graph in $\mathbb{R}^n$: two points are adjacent iff their
    50→Euclidean distance is exactly $1$. -/
    51→noncomputable def unitDistanceGraph (n : ℕ) : SimpleGraph (EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin n)) where
    52→  Adj x y := x ≠ y ∧ dist x y = 1
    53→  symm := fun _ _ ⟨hne, hd⟩ => ⟨hne.symm, by rw [dist_comm]; exact hd⟩
    54→  loopless := fun _ ⟨h, _⟩ => h rfl
    55→
    56→/-- The chromatic number of the unit distance graph in $\mathbb{R}^n$: the minimum number
    57→of colors needed to properly color all points so that no two points at
    58→distance $1$ share the same color. Returns $0$ if no finite coloring exists. -/
    59→noncomputable def chromaticNumber_unitDist (n : ℕ) : ℕ :=
    60→  sInf {k : ℕ | (unitDistanceGraph n).Colorable k}
    61→
    62→/--
    63→Erdős Problem 704, main open question:
    64→
    65→Does the limit $\lim_{n \to \infty} \chi(G_n)^{1/n}$ exist?
    66→-/
    67→@[category research open, AMS 5 52]
    68→theorem erdos_704 : answer(sorry) ↔
    69→    ∃ L : ℝ, Tendsto
    70→      (fun n : ℕ => (chromaticNumber_unitDist n : ℝ) ^ ((1 : ℝ) / (n : ℝ)))
    71→      atTop (nhds L) := by
    72→  sorry
    73→
    74→/--
    75→Erdős Problem 704, Frankl–Wilson lower bound [FrWi81]:
    76→
    77→The chromatic number of the unit distance graph in $\mathbb{R}^n$ grows at least
    78→exponentially: $\chi(G_n) \geq (1+o(1)) \cdot 1.2^n$.
    79→
    80→Formalized as: there exist $C > 0$ and $N_0$ such that for all $n \geq N_0$,
    81→$\chi(G_n) \geq C \cdot (6/5)^n$.
    82→-/
    83→@[category research solved, AMS 5 52]
    84→theorem erdos_704.variants.lower_bound :
    85→    ∃ C : ℝ, C > 0 ∧ ∃ N₀ : ℕ, ∀ n : ℕ, N₀ ≤ n →
    86→      (chromaticNumber_unitDist n : ℝ) ≥ C * ((6 : ℝ) / 5) ^ n := by
    87→  sorry
    88→
    89→/--
    90→Erdős Problem 704, Larman–Rogers upper bound [LaRo72]:
    91→
    92→$\chi(G_n) \leq (3+o(1))^n$.
    93→
    94→Formalized as: there exist $C > 0$ and $N_0$ such that for all $n \geq N_0$,
    95→$\chi(G_n) \leq C \cdot 3^n$.
    96→-/
    97→@[category research solved, AMS 5 52]
    98→theorem erdos_704.variants.upper_bound :
    99→    ∃ C : ℝ, C > 0 ∧ ∃ N₀ : ℕ, ∀ n : ℕ, N₀ ≤ n →
   100→      (chromaticNumber_unitDist n : ℝ) ≤ C * (3 : ℝ) ^ n := by
   101→  sorry
   102→
   103→end Erdos704
   104→

1→/-
     2→Copyright 2025 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

     5→you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

    12→WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    13→See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14→limitations under the License.
    15→-/
    16→
    17→import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Acyclic
    18→import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Bipartite
    19→import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Matching
    20→import Mathlib.Data.Real.Archimedean
    21→import Mathlib.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.PiL2
    22→
    23→namespace SimpleGraph
    24→
    25→open Classical Finset List
    26→
    27→variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [DecidableEq α]
    28→
    29→/-- `Ls G` is the maximum number of leaves over all spanning trees of `G`.
    30→It is defined to be `0` when `G` is not connected. -/
    31→noncomputable def Ls (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℝ :=
    32→  letI spanningTrees := { T : Subgraph G | T.IsSpanning ∧ IsTree T.coe }
    33→  letI leaves (T : Subgraph G) := T.verts.toFinset.filter (fun v => T.degree v = 1)
    34→  letI num_leaves (T : Subgraph G) := (leaves T).card
    35→  sSup (Set.image (fun T => (num_leaves T : ℝ)) spanningTrees)
    36→
    37→/-- `n G` is the number of vertices of `G` as a real number. -/
    38→noncomputable def n (_ : SimpleGraph α) : ℝ := Fintype.card α
    39→
    40→/-- `m G` is the size of a maximum matching of `G`. -/
    41→noncomputable def m (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℝ :=
    42→  let matchings := { M : Subgraph G | M.IsMatching }
    43→  sSup (Set.image (fun M => (M.edgeSet.toFinset.card : ℝ)) matchings)
    44→
    45→/-- The maximum cardinality among all independent sets `s`
    46→    that maximize the quantity `|s| - |N(s)|`, where `N(s)`
    47→    is the neighborhood of the set `s`. -/
    48→noncomputable def aprime (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℝ :=
    49→  letI indep_sets : Finset (Finset α) := univ.powerset.filter (fun s => G.IsIndepSet (s : Set α))
    50→  letI diff (s : Finset α) : ℤ := (s.card : ℤ) - (⋃ v ∈ (s : Set α), G.neighborSet v).toFinset.card
    51→  letI max_diff := (indep_sets.image diff).max
    52→  letI critical_sets := indep_sets.filter (fun s ↦ diff s = max_diff.getD 0)
    53→  letI max_card := (critical_sets.image Finset.card).max
    54→  (max_card.getD 0 : ℝ)
    55→
    56→/-- `largestInducedForestSize G` is the size of a largest induced forest of `G`. -/
    57→noncomputable def largestInducedForestSize (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℕ :=
    58→  sSup { n | ∃ s : Finset α, (G.induce s).IsAcyclic ∧ s.card = n }
    59→
    60→/-- The degree sequence of a graph, sorted in nondecreasing order. -/
    61→noncomputable def degreeSequence (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : List ℕ :=
    62→  (Finset.univ.val.map fun v : α => G.degree v).sort (· ≤ ·)
    63→
    64→/--
    65→The maximum number of occurrences of any term of the degree sequence of `G`.
    66→-/
    67→noncomputable def degreeSequenceMultiplicity (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℕ :=
    68→  letI degs := degreeSequence G
    69→  (List.max? (degs.map (fun d => degs.count d))).getD 0
    70→
    71→/-- `largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize G` is the size of a largest induced
    72→bipartite subgraph of `G`. -/
    73→noncomputable def largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℕ :=
    74→  sSup { n | ∃ s : Finset α, (G.induce s).IsBipartite ∧ s.card = n }
    75→
    76→/-- `b G` is the number of vertices of a largest induced bipartite subgraph of `G`.
    77→Returned as a real number. -/
    78→noncomputable def b (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℝ :=
    79→  (largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize G : ℝ)
    80→
    81→/-- Independence number of the neighbourhood of `v`. -/
    82→noncomputable def indepNeighborsCard (G : SimpleGraph α) (v : α) : ℕ :=
    83→  (G.induce (G.neighborSet v)).indepNum
    84→
    85→/-- The same quantity as a real number. -/
    86→noncomputable def indepNeighbors (G : SimpleGraph α) (v : α) : ℝ :=
    87→  (indepNeighborsCard G v : ℝ)
    88→
    89→/-- Average of `indepNeighbors` over all vertices. -/
    90→noncomputable def averageIndepNeighbors (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℝ :=
    91→  (∑ v ∈ Finset.univ, indepNeighbors G v) / (Fintype.card α : ℝ)
    92→
    93→/-- A unit distance graph in ℝ²:
    94→A graph where the vertices V are a collection of points in ℝ² and there is
    95→an edge between two points if and only if the distance between them is 1. -/
    96→def UnitDistancePlaneGraph (V : Set (EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 2))) : SimpleGraph V where
    97→  Adj x y := Dist.dist x y = 1
    98→  symm x y := by simp [PseudoMetricSpace.dist_comm]
    99→
   100→/--
   101→Two walks are internally disjoint if they share no vertices other than their endpoints.
   102→-/
   103→def InternallyDisjoint {V : Type*} {G : SimpleGraph V} {u v x y : V}
   104→    (p : G.Walk u v) (q : G.Walk x y) : Prop :=
   105→  Disjoint p.support.tail.dropLast q.support.tail.dropLast
   106→
   107→/--
   108→We say a graph is infinitely connected if any two vertices are connected by infinitely many
   109→pairwise disjoint paths.
   110→-/
   111→def InfinitelyConnected {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : Prop :=
   112→  Pairwise fun u v ↦ ∃ P : Set (G.Walk u v),
   113→    P.Infinite ∧ (∀ p ∈ P, p.IsPath) ∧ P.Pairwise InternallyDisjoint
   114→
   115→/-- Infinite graphs: definitions for max degree and clique number so that the maximum
   116→degree (resp. clique number) of a graph with unbounded degree (resp. clique size) is
   117→`∞` rather than 0.
   118→-/
   119→noncomputable
   120→def edegree {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (v : V) : ℕ∞ := (G.neighborSet v).encard
   121→
   122→noncomputable
   123→def emaxDegree {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : ℕ∞ := ⨆ v, G.edegree v
   124→
   125→noncomputable
   126→def ecliqueNum {V : Type} (G : SimpleGraph V) : ℕ∞ := ⨆ (s : Finset V) (_ : G.IsClique s), #s
   127→
   128→end SimpleGraph
   129→

    16→import FormalConjecturesForMathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.GraphConjectures.Definitions
    17→import FormalConjecturesForMathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.GraphConjectures.Domination
    18→import Mathlib.Analysis.Matrix.Spectrum
    19→import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.AdjMatrix
    20→import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Metric
    21→import Mathlib.Data.Multiset.Interval
    22→
    23→noncomputable def Matrix.IsHermitian.maxEigenvalue {𝕜 : Type*} [Field 𝕜] [RCLike 𝕜]
    24→    {n : Type*} [Fintype n] [DecidableEq n] {A : Matrix n n 𝕜} (hA : A.IsHermitian) : ℝ :=
    25→  iSup hA.eigenvalues
    26→
    27→namespace SimpleGraph
    28→
    29→open Classical
    30→
    31→variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [DecidableEq α]
    32→
    33→/-- Abbreviation for the average independence number of the neighborhoods.
    34→-/
    35→noncomputable abbrev l (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℝ := averageIndepNeighbors G
    36→
    37→/-- The same quantity under a different name, used in some conjectures.
    38→-/
    39→noncomputable abbrev l_avg (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℝ := averageIndepNeighbors G
    40→
    41→/-- Independent domination number of `G`. -/
    42→noncomputable def gi (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℕ := G.indepDominationNumber
    43→
    44→/-- `temp_v G v = deg(v)/(n(G) - deg(v))`. -/
    45→noncomputable def temp_v (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] (v : α) : ℝ :=
    46→  let n := Fintype.card α
    47→  let deg := G.degree v
    48→  if n = deg then 0 else (deg : ℝ) / ((n : ℝ) - (deg : ℝ))
    49→
    50→/-- Maximum of `temp_v` over all vertices. -/
    51→noncomputable def MaxTemp (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] : ℝ :=
    52→  let temps := Finset.univ.image (temp_v G)
    53→  temps.max' (Finset.image_nonempty.mpr Finset.univ_nonempty)
    54→
    55→/-- Cardinality of the union of the neighbourhoods of the ends of the non-edge `e`. -/
    56→def non_edge_neighborhood_card (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] (e : Sym2 α) : ℕ :=
    57→  Sym2.lift ⟨fun u v => (G.neighborFinset u ∪ G.neighborFinset v).card,
    58→    fun u v => by simp [Finset.union_comm]⟩ e
    59→
    60→/-- Minimum size of the neighbourhood of a non-edge of `G`. -/
    61→noncomputable def NG (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℝ :=
    62→  let non_edges := (compl G).edgeFinset
    63→  if h : non_edges.Nonempty then
    64→    let neighbor_sizes := non_edges.image (non_edge_neighborhood_card G)
    65→    (neighbor_sizes.min' (Finset.Nonempty.image h _))
    66→  else
    67→    (Fintype.card α : ℝ)
    68→
    69→/-- Size of a largest induced forest of `G` expressed as a real number. -/
    70→noncomputable def S (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℝ :=
    71→  let card := Fintype.card α
    72→  if card < 2 then 0 else
    73→    let degrees := Multiset.ofList (List.map (fun v => G.degree v) Finset.univ.toList)
    74→    let sorted_degrees := degrees.sort (· ≤ ·)
    75→    ↑((sorted_degrees[card - 2]?).getD 0)
    76→
    77→/-- Local eccentricity of a vertex. -/
    78→noncomputable def local_eccentricity (G : SimpleGraph α) (v : α) : ENat :=
    79→  sSup (Set.range (G.edist v))
    80→
    81→/-- The largest integer less than or equal to `x`. -/
    82→noncomputable def FLOOR (x : ℝ) : ℝ := ⌊x⌋
    83→
    84→/-- Eccentricity of a vertex. -/
    85→noncomputable def eccentricity (G : SimpleGraph α) (v : α) : ℕ∞ :=
    86→  sSup (Set.range (G.edist v))
    87→
    88→/-- The minimum eccentricity among all vertices. -/
    89→noncomputable def minEccentricity (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℕ∞ :=
    90→  sInf (Set.range (eccentricity G))
    91→
    92→/-- The set of vertices of minimum eccentricity. -/
    93→noncomputable def graphCenter (G : SimpleGraph α) : Set α :=
    94→  {v : α | eccentricity G v = minEccentricity G}
    95→
    96→/-- The maximum eccentricity among all vertices. -/
    97→noncomputable def maxEccentricity (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℕ∞ :=
    98→  sSup (Set.range (eccentricity G))
    99→
   100→/-- The set of vertices of maximum eccentricity. -/
   101→noncomputable def maxEccentricityVertices (G : SimpleGraph α) : Set α :=
   102→  {v : α | eccentricity G v = maxEccentricity G}
   103→
   104→/-- Distance from a vertex to a finite set. -/
   105→noncomputable def distToSet (G : SimpleGraph α) (v : α) (S : Set α) : ℕ :=
   106→  if h : S.toFinset.Nonempty then
   107→    (S.toFinset.image (fun s => G.dist v s)).min' (Finset.Nonempty.image h _)
   108→  else 0
   109→
   110→/-- Average distance of `G`. -/
   111→noncomputable def averageDistance (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℝ :=
   112→  if Fintype.card α > 1 then
   113→    (∑ u ∈ Finset.univ, ∑ v ∈ Finset.univ, (G.dist u v : ℝ)) /
   114→      ((Fintype.card α : ℝ) * ((Fintype.card α : ℝ) - 1))
   115→  else
   116→    0
   117→
   118→/-- The floor of the average distance of `G`. -/
   119→noncomputable def path (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℕ :=
   120→  if G.Connected then
   121→    Nat.floor (averageDistance G)
   122→  else
   123→    0
   124→
   125→/-- Auxiliary quantity `ecc` used in conjecture 34. -/
   126→noncomputable def ecc (G : SimpleGraph α) (S : Set α) : ℕ :=
   127→  let s_comp := Finset.univ.filter (fun v => v ∉ S)
   128→  if h : s_comp.Nonempty then
   129→    (s_comp.image (fun v => distToSet G v S)).max' (Finset.Nonempty.image h _)
   130→  else 0
   131→
   132→/-- Average distance from all vertices to a given set. -/
   133→noncomputable def distavg (G : SimpleGraph α) (S : Set α) : ℝ :=
   134→  if Fintype.card α > 0 then
   135→    (∑ v ∈ Finset.univ, (distToSet G v S : ℝ)) / (Fintype.card α : ℝ)
   136→  else
   137→    0
   138→
   139→/-- A family of paths covering all vertices without overlaps. -/
   140→def IsPathCover (G : SimpleGraph α) (P : Finset (Finset α)) : Prop :=
   141→  (∀ s1 ∈ P, ∀ s2 ∈ P, s1 ≠ s2 → Disjoint s1 s2) ∧
   142→  (Finset.univ ⊆ P.biUnion id) ∧
   143→  (∀ s ∈ P, ∃ (u v : α) (p : G.Walk u v), p.IsPath ∧ s = p.support.toFinset)
   144→
   145→/-- Minimum size of a path cover of `G`. -/
   146→noncomputable def pathCoverNumber (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℕ :=
   147→  sInf { k | ∃ P : Finset (Finset α), P.card = k ∧ IsPathCover G P }
   148→
   149→/-- The same quantity as a real number. -/
   150→noncomputable def p (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℝ := (pathCoverNumber G : ℝ)
   151→
   152→/-- The Wiener index of `G`, which is the sum of distances between all
   153→pairs of vertices. -/
   154→noncomputable def wienerIndex (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℕ :=
   155→  ∑ uv : Sym2 α, uv.lift ⟨fun u v ↦ G.dist u v, by simp [dist_comm]⟩
   156→
   157→/-- Auxiliary function for Szeged index: counts vertices closer to u than v. -/
   158→noncomputable def szeged_aux (G : SimpleGraph α) (u v : α) : ℕ :=
   159→  (Finset.univ.filter (fun w => G.edist w u < G.edist w v)).card
   160→
   161→/-- The Szeged index of `G`.
   162→
   163→This is define as the sum `∑_{uv ∈ E(G)} n_u(u,v) * n_v(u,v)` where
   164→`n_u(uv)` is the number of vertices closer to `u` than `v`.
   165→-/
   166→noncomputable def szegedIndex (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℕ :=
   167→  ∑ e ∈ G.edgeFinset,
   168→    e.lift ⟨fun u v => szeged_aux G u v * szeged_aux G v u, by simp [mul_comm]⟩
   169→
   170→/-- The average degree of `G`. -/
   171→noncomputable def averageDegree (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℚ  :=
   172→  (∑ v, (G.degree v : ℚ)) / (Fintype.card α : ℚ)
   173→
   174→/-- The multiset of degrees of a graph. -/
   175→def degreeMultiset (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : Multiset ℕ :=
   176→  Finset.univ.val.map fun v => G.degree v
   177→
   178→/-- The annihilation number of a graph. This is the largest number of degrees that can be added
   179→together without going over the total number of edges of that graph. -/
   180→def annihilationNumber (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℕ :=
   181→  -- Calculate the limit: The number of edges (Sum of degrees / 2)
   182→  letI limit := G.edgeFinset.card
   183→
   184→  -- The set of all multisets of degrees that sum to less than or equal to `limit`
   185→  Finset.Iic (degreeMultiset G)
   186→    |>.filter (fun S ↦ Multiset.sum S ≤ limit)
   187→    |>.sup Multiset.card
   188→
   189→/--
   190→Computes the annihilation number of a graph G.
   191→
   192→It calculates the degree sequence, sorts it ascendingly, and finds the largest prefix length 'k'
   193→(where `0 ≤ k ≤ |V(G)|`) such that the sum of the prefix is less than or equal to the sum of the corresponding suffix.
   194→-/
   195→noncomputable def annihilationNumber' (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℕ :=
   196→  -- 1. Get the degree sequence sorted in ascending order.
   197→  -- G.degree_list returns the list of degrees.
   198→  letI degrees := (Finset.univ.image fun v => G.degree v).sort (· ≤ ·)
   199→
   200→  -- 2. Define the condition for the annihilation number.
   201→  -- k represents the number of smallest degrees considered (the length of the prefix).
   202→  letI condition (k : ℕ) : Bool := (degrees.take k).sum ≤ (degrees.drop k).sum
   203→
   204→  -- 3. Find the maximum k in {0, ..., n} satisfying the condition.
   205→  -- List.range (n + 1) generates the list [0, 1, ..., n].
   206→  letI candidates := (List.range (Fintype.card α + 1)).filter condition
   207→
   208→  -- 4. Return the maximum candidate.
   209→  -- The list of candidates is guaranteed to be non-empty because k=0 always satisfies
   210→  -- the condition (0 ≤ sum of all degrees).
   211→  candidates.getLast!
   212→
   213→set_option linter.unusedSectionVars false in
   214→-- TODO(Paul-Lez): debug the issue with the unused variable linter...
   215→proof_wanted annihilationNumberEq (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] :
   216→    annihilationNumber G = annihilationNumber' G
   217→
   218→/-!
   219→## Residue
   220→The residue of a graph is the number of zeros remaining after iteratively applying the Havel-Hakimi
   221→algorithm to the degree sequence until all remaining degrees are zero.
   222→-/
   223→
   224→/--
   225→Helper function: Performs one step of the Havel-Hakimi reduction on a degree sequence.
   226→Assumes the input list `s` is sorted descending.
   227→Removes the first element `d`, decrements the next `d` elements by 1, and re-sorts the list descending.
   228→
   229→Note: when `s` is the list of vertices arising from a simple graph, if the first index is `s` then
   230→the degree list always has length at least `s+1` so this makes sense.
   231→-/
   232→private def havelHakimiStep (s : List ℕ) : List ℕ :=
   233→  match s with
   234→  | [] => []
   235→  | d :: rest =>
   236→    -- Split the rest into the part to decrement (first d elements) and the remaining part.
   237→    let (to_decrement, remaining) := rest.splitAt d
   238→    -- Decrement the elements
   239→    let decremented := to_decrement.map (· - 1)
   240→    -- Combine and re-sort descending.
   241→    (decremented ++ remaining).mergeSort (· ≥ ·)
   242→
   243→/--
   244→Auxiliary function to calculate the residue recursively.
   245→Applies Havel-Hakimi steps until the sequence consists only of zeros or is empty.
   246→-/
   247→private partial def residueAux : List ℕ → ℕ
   248→  | [] => 0        -- Empty sequence, residue is 0.
   249→  | 0 :: s => 1 + s.length -- If the largest degree is 0 (and the list is sorted), all are 0.
   250→  | s => residueAux (havelHakimiStep s) -- Apply one reduction step and recurse.
   251→
   252→/--
   253→Computes the residue of a graph G, ,i.e. the number of zeros remaining after iteratively applying the Havel-Hakimi
   254→algorithm to the degree sequence until all remaining degrees are zero.
   255→Starts with the descending degree sequence and applies the Havel-Hakimi process.
   256→-/
   257→noncomputable def residue (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℕ :=
   258→  -- Get the degree sequence sorted in descending order and apply `residueAux`.
   259→  residueAux ((Finset.univ.image fun v => G.degree v).sort (· ≥ ·))
   260→
   261→/--
   262→Fractional alpha. This is defined as
   263→$$
   264→\max_x \sum_{v \in V} x_v
   265→$$
   266→subject to $0 \le x_v \le 1$ for all $v \in V$ and
   267→$x_u + x_v \le 1$ whenever $(u, v)$ are connected by an edge.
   268→-/
   269→noncomputable def fractionalAlpha (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℝ :=
   270→  sSup {(∑ i, x i) | (x : α → ℝ) (_ : ∀ v, x v ∈ Set.Icc 0 1)
   271→    (_ : ∀ u v, G.Adj u v → x u + x v ≤ 1)}
   272→
   273→/--
   274→Lovász Theta Function (ϑ(G))
   275→The Lovász theta function is defined as:
   276→ϑ(G) = min λ_max(A)
   277→where the minimum is taken over all symmetric matrices A such that:
   278→
   279→A_ij = 1 for all i = j (diagonal entries are 1)
   280→A_ij = 0 for all {i,j} ∈ E (entries corresponding to edges are 0)
   281→A is positive semidefinite
   282→
   283→Here λ_max(A) denotes the maximum eigenvalue of A.
   284→-/
   285→noncomputable def lovaczThetaFunction
   286→    (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℝ :=
   287→  sInf {(Matrix.IsHermitian.maxEigenvalue hA) | (A : Matrix α α ℝ) (hA : A.IsHermitian)
   288→      (_ : ∀ i, A i i = 1) (_ : ∀ i j, G.Adj i j → A i j = 0)}
   289→
   290→/--
   291→Given a simple graph `G` with adjacency matrix `A`, this is the number `n₀ + min n₊ n₋` where:
   292→- `n₀` is the multiplicity of zero as an eigenvalue of `A`
   293→- `n₊` is the number of positive eigenvalues of `A` (counting multiplicities)
   294→- `n₋` is the number of negative eigenvalues of `A` (counting multiplicities)
   295→-/
   296→noncomputable def cvetkovic (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℕ :=
   297→  letI A : Matrix α α ℝ := G.adjMatrix ℝ
   298→  letI spectrum : Multiset ℝ := (Matrix.charpoly A).roots
   299→  letI positive_count : ℕ := spectrum.countP (fun x => 0 < x)
   300→  letI negative_count : ℕ := spectrum.countP (fun x => 0 > x)
   301→  letI zero_count : ℕ := spectrum.countP (fun x => 0 = x)
   302→  zero_count + min positive_count negative_count
   303→
   304→end SimpleGraph
   305→

1→/-

    14→limitations under the License.
    15→-/
    16→
    17→import FormalConjecturesForMathlib.Algebra.Order.Group.Pointwise.Interval
    18→import FormalConjecturesForMathlib.Data.Set.Bdd
    19→import FormalConjecturesForMathlib.Order.Interval.Finset.Basic
    20→import FormalConjecturesForMathlib.Order.Interval.Finset.Nat
    21→import Batteries.Util.ProofWanted
    22→
    23→open Filter
    24→
    25→open scoped Topology
    26→
    27→namespace Set
    28→
    29→/--
    30→Given a set `S` and an element `b` in an order `β`, where all intervals bounded above are finite,
    31→we define the partial density of `S` (relative to a set `A`) to be the proportion of elements in
    32→`{x ∈ A | x < b}` that lie in `S ∩ A`.
    33→
    34→This definition was inspired from https://github.com/b-mehta/unit-fractions
    35→-/
    36→@[inline]
    37→noncomputable abbrev partialDensity {β : Type*} [Preorder β] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot β]
    38→    (S : Set β) (A : Set β := Set.univ) (b : β) : ℝ :=
    39→  (Set.interIio (S ∩ A) b).ncard / (Set.interIio A b).ncard
    40→
    41→theorem partialDensity_le_one {β : Type*} [Preorder β] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot β]
    42→    (S : Set β) (A : Set β := Set.univ) (b : β) : S.partialDensity A b ≤ 1 := by
    43→  apply div_le_one_of_le₀ _ (Nat.cast_nonneg _)
    44→  exact mod_cast Set.ncard_le_ncard <| Set.inter_subset_inter_left _ inter_subset_right
    45→
    46→/--
    47→Given a set `S` in an order `β`, where all intervals bounded above are finite, we define the upper
    48→density of `S` (relative to a set `A`) to be the limsup of the partial densities of `S`
    49→(relative to `A`) for `b → ∞`.
    50→-/
    51→noncomputable def upperDensity {β : Type*} [Preorder β] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot β]
    52→    (S : Set β) (A : Set β := Set.univ) : ℝ :=
    53→  atTop.limsup fun (b : β) ↦ S.partialDensity A b
    54→
    55→/--
    56→Given a set `S` in an order `β`, where all intervals bounded above are finite, we define the lower
    57→density of `S` (relative to a set `A`) to be the liminf of the partial densities of `S`
    58→(relative to `A`) for `b → ∞`.
    59→-/
    60→noncomputable def lowerDensity {β : Type*} [Preorder β] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot β]
    61→    (S : Set β) (A : Set β := Set.univ) : ℝ :=
    62→  atTop.liminf fun (b : β) ↦ S.partialDensity A b
    63→
    64→theorem lowerDensity_le_one {β : Type*} [Preorder β] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot β]
    65→    (S : Set β) (A : Set β := Set.univ) : S.lowerDensity A ≤ 1 := by
    66→  by_cases h : atTop (α := β) = ⊥
    67→  · simp [h, Set.lowerDensity, Filter.liminf_eq]
    68→  · have : (atTop (α := β)).NeBot := ⟨h⟩
    69→    apply Real.sSup_le (fun x hx ↦ ?_) one_pos.le
    70→    simpa using hx.mono fun y hy ↦ hy.trans (Set.partialDensity_le_one _ _ y)
    71→
    72→theorem lowerDensity_nonneg {β : Type*} [Preorder β] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot β]
    73→    (S : Set β) (A : Set β := Set.univ) : 0 ≤ S.lowerDensity A := by
    74→  rw [Set.lowerDensity, Filter.liminf_eq]
    75→  exact (em _).elim (le_csSup · <| .of_forall fun _ ↦ by positivity)
    76→    (Real.sSup_of_not_bddAbove · |>.ge)
    77→
    78→/--
    79→A set `S` in an order `β` where all intervals bounded above are finite is said to have
    80→density `α : ℝ` (relative to a set `A`) if the proportion of `x ∈ S` such that `x < n`
    81→in `A` tends to `α` as `n → ∞`.
    82→
    83→When `β = ℕ` this by default defines the natural density of a set
    84→(i.e., relative to all of `ℕ`).
    85→-/
    86→def HasDensity {β : Type*} [Preorder β] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot β]
    87→    (S : Set β) (α : ℝ) (A : Set β := Set.univ) : Prop :=
    88→  Tendsto (fun (b : β) => S.partialDensity A b) atTop (𝓝 α)
    89→
    90→/--
    91→A set `S` in an order `β` where all intervals bounded above are finite is said to have
    92→positive density (relative to a set `A`) if there exists a positive `α : ℝ` such that
    93→`S` has density `α` (relative to a set `A`).
    94→-/
    95→def HasPosDensity {β : Type*} [Preorder β] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot β]
    96→    (S : Set β) (A : Set β := Set.univ) : Prop :=
    97→  ∃ α > 0, S.HasDensity α A
    98→
    99→namespace HasDensity
   100→
   101→-- TODO(mercuris): generalise these to non-univ `A`
   102→
   103→/-- In a non-trivial partial order with a least element, the set of all
   104→elements has density one. -/
   105→@[simp]
   106→theorem univ {β : Type*} [PartialOrder β] [LocallyFiniteOrder β] [OrderBot β] [Nontrivial β] :
   107→    (@Set.univ β).HasDensity 1 := by
   108→  by_cases h : atTop (α := β) = ⊥
   109→  · simp [h, HasDensity]
   110→  · simp [HasDensity, partialDensity]
   111→    let ⟨b, hb⟩ := Set.Iio_eventually_ncard_ne_zero β
   112→    refine tendsto_const_nhds.congr' ?_
   113→    exact (eventually_ge_atTop b).mono fun n hn ↦ (div_self <| mod_cast hb n hn).symm
   114→
   115→theorem univ_nat_hasDensity_one : (@Set.univ ℕ).HasDensity 1 := univ
   116→
   117→@[simp]
   118→theorem empty {β : Type*} [Preorder β] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot β] (A : Set β := Set.univ) :
   119→    Set.HasDensity (∅ : Set β) 0 A := by
   120→  simpa [HasDensity, partialDensity] using tendsto_const_nhds
   121→
   122→theorem mono {β : Type*} [PartialOrder β] [LocallyFiniteOrder β] [OrderBot β]
   123→    {S T : Set β} {αS αT : ℝ} [(atTop (α := β)).NeBot] (h : S ⊆ T) (hS : S.HasDensity αS)
   124→    (hT : T.HasDensity αT) : αS ≤ αT := by
   125→  rw [HasDensity] at hS hT
   126→  apply le_of_tendsto_of_tendsto hS hT
   127→  filter_upwards [eventually_ge_atTop ⊥] with b hb
   128→  apply div_le_div_of_nonneg_right
   129→  grw [Set.ncard_le_ncard (inter_subset_inter_left _ (inter_subset_inter_left _ h))]
   130→  exact Nat.cast_nonneg _
   131→
   132→theorem nonneg {β : Type*} [Preorder β] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot β] [(atTop : Filter β).NeBot]
   133→    {S : Set β} {α : ℝ} (h : S.HasDensity α) : 0 ≤ α :=
   134→  le_of_tendsto_of_tendsto' empty h fun b => by simp [div_nonneg, partialDensity]
   135→
   136→end Set.HasDensity
   137→
   138→namespace Nat
   139→
   140→open Set
   141→
   142→/--
   143→The natural density of the set of even numbers is `1 / 2`.
   144→-/
   145→theorem hasDensity_even : {n : ℕ | Even n}.HasDensity (1 / 2) := by
   146→  simp [HasDensity, partialDensity, Set.interIio]
   147→  have h {n : ℕ} (hn : 1 ≤ n) : (({n : ℕ | Even n} ∩ Iio n).ncard : ℝ) / n =
   148→      if Even n then 2⁻¹ else (n + 1 : ℝ) /  n * 2⁻¹ := by
   149→    split_ifs with h
   150→    · rw [← image_mul_two_Iio_even h, ncard_image_of_injective _
   151→          (mul_right_injective₀ (by simp)), ncard_Iio,
   152→        cast_div_charZero (even_iff_two_dvd.mp h), cast_ofNat,
   153→        div_div_cancel_left' <| cast_ne_zero.2 (by linarith)]
   154→    · replace h : Even (n + 1) := by simpa [Nat.even_add', ← Nat.not_even_iff_odd]
   155→      rw [← image_mul_two_Iio n, ncard_image_of_injective _
   156→          (mul_right_injective₀ (by simp)), ncard_Iio,
   157→        cast_div (even_iff_two_dvd.mp h) (by norm_num), cast_add]; ring
   158→  refine Tendsto.congr' (eventually_atTop.2 ⟨1, fun n hn => (h hn).symm⟩)
   159→    (Tendsto.if' tendsto_const_nhds ?_)
   160→  replace h : Tendsto (fun (k : ℕ) => 1 + 1 / (k : ℝ)) atTop (𝓝 1) := by
   161→    simpa using Tendsto.const_add (M := ℝ) _ tendsto_one_div_atTop_nhds_zero_nat
   162→  simpa using Tendsto.mul_const _ <|
   163→    Tendsto.congr' (eventually_atTop.2 ⟨1, fun k hk => by field_simp⟩) h
   164→
   165→/-- A finite set has natural density zero. -/
   166→theorem hasDensity_zero_of_finite {S : Set ℕ} (h : S.Finite) : S.HasDensity 0 := by
   167→  simp [HasDensity, partialDensity, Set.interIio]
   168→  have (n : ℕ) : ((S ∩ Set.Iio n).ncard : ℝ) / n ≤ S.ncard / n := by
   169→    by_cases h₀ : n = 0; simp [h₀]
   170→    exact div_le_div₀ (by simp) (by simpa using Set.ncard_inter_le_ncard_left _ _ h)
   171→      (by simpa using n.pos_of_ne_zero h₀) le_rfl
   172→  exact tendsto_of_tendsto_of_tendsto_of_le_of_le tendsto_const_nhds
   173→    (tendsto_const_div_atTop_nhds_zero_nat _)
   174→    (fun _ => div_nonneg (cast_nonneg _) (cast_nonneg _)) this
   175→
   176→/-- A set of positive natural density is infinite. -/
   177→theorem infinite_of_hasDensity_pos {S : Set ℕ} {α : ℝ} (h : S.HasDensity α) (hα : α ≠ 0) :
   178→    S.Infinite :=
   179→  mt hasDensity_zero_of_finite fun h' => hα (tendsto_nhds_unique h h')
   180→
   181→end Nat
   182→
   183→/-! ## Logarithmic Density -/
   184→
   185→section LogarithmicDensity
   186→
   187→open Finset Real Classical
   188→
   189→/--
   190→A set `A` of natural numbers has logarithmic density `d` if the sequence
   191→$(1 / \log n) \cdot \sum_{k \in A, k \le n} (1/k)$ converges to `d`.
   192→Logarithmic density is a weaker notion than natural density: if a set has natural density `d`,
   193→then it also has logarithmic density `d` (see `Set.HasDensity.hasLogDensity`), but the converse
   194→is false.
   195→-/
   196→def Set.HasLogDensity (A : Set ℕ) (d : ℝ) : Prop :=
   197→  Tendsto (fun n : ℕ => (∑ k ≤ n with k ∈ A, (k : ℝ)⁻¹ / .log n : ℝ)) atTop (𝓝 d)
   198→
   199→/-- If a set has natural density `d`, then it also has logarithmic density `d`. -/
   200→proof_wanted Set.HasDensity.hasLogDensity {A : Set ℕ} {d : ℝ} (h : A.HasDensity d) : A.HasLogDensity d
   201→
   202→end LogarithmicDensity
   203→
